description: Canonical command catalog for the Unity Editor MCP bridge. Authoritative source of the command surface shared by the Node server and the Unity editor package.

commands.name: set_project_setting
commands.description: Set one project setting by key (PlayerSettings). Supported keys: productName, companyName, bundleVersion, defaultScreenWidth, defaultScreenHeight, runInBackground, colorSpace, scriptingDefineSymbols.

value.description: New value; type depends on the key (string, number, or boolean).

commands.name: manage_packages
commands.description: Add or remove a UPM package. Resolution is asynchronous (the editor recompiles/reloads); the call returns once the request is queued — verify with list_packages afterwards.

action.description: Whether to add or remove the package.

packageId.description: Package identifier, e.g. "com.unity.textmeshpro" or "com.unity.textmeshpro@3.0.6" or a git URL.

commands.name: quit_editor
commands.description: Quit the Unity editor (intended for CI/automation). The editor exits after the response is sent, so the connection will drop. Unsaved changes are NOT saved.

commands.name: get_symbols
commands.description: Outline a C# file: its types (class/struct/interface/enum), methods, and properties with line ranges. Syntactic (no semantic resolution). Accepts an "Assets/..." path or an absolute .cs path under the project.

path.description: Path to the .cs file ("Assets/..." or absolute).

commands.name: find_symbol
commands.description: Find C# symbol declarations by exact name across the project Assets scripts. Returns each match's file, line range, kind, and signature. Syntactic (no semantic resolution).

kind.description: Optional: only return declarations of this kind.

commands.name: find_references
commands.description: Find textual references to an identifier across the project Assets scripts (comments and string literals excluded). Syntactic: same-named members across types/overloads are not disambiguated. Returns file, line, and the matching line text.

resolution.description: How references were computed: "syntactic" (textual, lite) or "semantic" (Roslyn sidecar). Lite always returns "syntactic".

commands.name: get_symbol_body
commands.description: Return the source text of a named symbol (type/method/property) within a C# file — the declaration through its matching closing brace. Syntactic. Accepts an "Assets/..." path or an absolute .cs path under the project.

path.description: Path to the .cs file ("Assets/..." or absolute).

name.description: Exact symbol name within the file.

commands.name: resolve_symbol
commands.description: Resolve a C# identifier NAME to its declaring type(s) and member(s) using Unity's compiled assemblies (reflection/TypeCache). Name-based: returns a ranked candidate list — same-named symbols across types are NOT disambiguated (that needs the Roslyn sidecar). Pass "name", or "path"+"position" to extract the token name at that source location.

name.description: Identifier name to resolve. Optional if path+position are given.

path.description: A .cs file under the project, used with position to extract the token name.

position.description: { line, column } (1-based) of the token in path.

commands.name: get_type_members
commands.description: List the members (fields, properties, methods, events) of a named C# type from Unity's compiled assemblies, with signatures, visibility, and attributes.

typeName.description: Simple or full type name (e.g. "Player" or "MyGame.Player").

commands.name: find_implementations
commands.description: Find subtypes of a named class or implementors of a named interface, via UnityEditor.TypeCache (fast, indexed over the compiled assemblies).

commands.name: get_editor_info
commands.description: Get Unity editor environment info: Unity version, platform, project path, active build target, product/company name, and quick play/compile state.

commands.name: get_project_settings
commands.description: Get key Unity project settings: product/company name, bundle version, color space, default screen size, scripting backend, API compatibility level, scripting define symbols, and the active build target.

commands.name: list_packages
commands.description: List the Unity project UPM packages: directly-requested dependencies (from manifest.json) plus the full resolved set with each package source (from packages-lock.json).

commands.name: analyze_scene_contents
commands.description: Analyze and get statistics about the current scene

includeInactive.description: Include inactive objects in analysis. Default: true

groupByType.description: Group results by component types. Default: true

includePrefabInfo.description: Include prefab connection info. Default: true

includeMemoryInfo.description: Include memory usage estimates. Default: false

componentDistribution.description: Count of each component type in scene, with Scripts as nested object

memoryInfo.description: Only present if includeMemoryInfo was true

commands.name: find_by_component
commands.description: Find all GameObjects that have a specific component type

searchScope.description: Where to search: current scene, prefabs, or all. Default: "scene"

matchExactType.description: Match exact type only (not derived types). Default: true

componentTypes.description: Only present if matchExactType was false

commands.name: get_component_values
commands.description: Get all properties and values of a specific component

componentIndex.description: Index if multiple components of same type. Default: 0

enabled.description: Component enabled state (true if component doesn't support enabled property)

type.description: Type name: float, int, bool, string, Vector3, Vector2, Color, Quaternion, enum name, etc.

range.description: Only for numeric properties with RangeAttribute

commands.name: get_gameobject_details
commands.description: Get detailed information about a specific GameObject

gameObjectName.description: Name of the GameObject to inspect

path.description: Full hierarchy path to the GameObject (use either name or path)

includeChildren.description: Include full hierarchy details. Default: false

includeComponents.description: Include all component details. Default: true

maxDepth.description: Maximum depth for child traversal. Default: 3, Range: 0-10

components.description: Component details including type, enabled, and properties; only present if includeComponents=true

children.description: Child object details recursively; only present if includeChildren=true and maxDepth > 0

commands.name: get_object_references
commands.description: Find all references to and from a GameObject

gameObjectName.description: Name of the GameObject to analyze references for

includeAssetReferences.description: Include references to assets (materials, meshes, etc). Default: true

includeHierarchyReferences.description: Include parent/child hierarchy references. Default: true

searchInPrefabs.description: Also search for references in prefab assets. Default: false

isPrefab.description: Only present if target is a prefab

referenceType.description: direct, transform, component, array, list, hierarchy, etc.

prefabInstances.description: Only present if target isPrefab and prefabInstances > 0

circularReferences.description: Only present if circular references detected

commands.name: analyze_asset_dependencies
commands.description: Analyze Unity asset dependencies (get dependencies, dependents, circular deps, unused assets, size impact)

assetPath.description: Path to the asset (required for specific asset analysis)

recursive.description: Whether to analyze dependencies recursively (for get_dependencies)

includeBuiltIn.description: Whether to include built-in assets in analysis (for find_unused)

commands.name: create_material
commands.description: Create a new material in Unity with specified shader and properties

materialPath.description: Asset path for the material (must start with Assets/ and end with .mat)

copyFrom.description: Path to existing material to copy from

commands.name: create_prefab
commands.description: Create a new prefab from a GameObject or from scratch

gameObjectPath.description: Path to GameObject to convert to prefab

prefabPath.description: Asset path where prefab should be saved (must start with Assets/ and end with .prefab)

createFromTemplate.description: Create empty prefab without source GameObject

overwrite.description: Overwrite existing prefab if it exists

commands.name: exit_prefab_mode
commands.description: Exit prefab mode and return to the main scene

commands.name: instantiate_prefab
commands.description: Instantiate a prefab in the scene

prefabPath.description: Asset path to the prefab (must start with Assets/ and end with .prefab)

position.description: World position for the instantiated object

name.description: Override name for the instantiated object

commands.name: manage_asset_database
commands.description: Manage Unity Asset Database operations (find, info, create folders, move, copy, delete, refresh)

searchInFolders.description: Folders to search in for find_assets (optional)

assetPath.description: Path to the asset (must start with "Assets/")

folderPath.description: Path for folder creation (must start with "Assets/")

commands.name: manage_asset_import_settings
commands.description: Manage Unity asset import settings (get, modify, apply presets, reimport)

assetPath.description: Path to the asset (must start with "Assets/")

settings.description: Import settings to apply (required for modify action)

preset.description: Name of the preset to apply (required for apply_preset action)

commands.name: modify_material
commands.description: Modify properties of an existing material

materialPath.description: Asset path to the material (must start with Assets/ and end with .mat)

commands.name: modify_prefab
commands.description: Modify properties of an existing prefab

prefabPath.description: Asset path to the prefab (must start with Assets/ and end with .prefab)

applyToInstances.description: Apply changes to scene instances of the prefab

commands.name: open_prefab
commands.description: Open a prefab asset in prefab mode for editing

prefabPath.description: Asset path to the prefab (must start with Assets/ and end with .prefab)

focusObject.description: Optional path to object within prefab to focus on (relative to prefab root)

isolateObject.description: Isolate the focused object in the hierarchy (default: false)

commands.name: save_prefab
commands.description: Save current prefab changes in prefab mode or save a GameObject as prefab override

gameObjectPath.description: Path to GameObject to save as prefab override (optional - if not provided, saves current prefab in prefab mode)

includeChildren.description: Include child object overrides when saving (default: true)

commands.name: get_compilation_state
commands.description: Get current Unity compilation state, errors, and warnings with enhanced detection

maxMessages.description: Maximum number of messages to return (default: 50)

commands.name: start_compilation_monitoring
commands.description: Start monitoring Unity compilation events and error detection

commands.name: stop_compilation_monitoring
commands.description: Stop monitoring Unity compilation events

commands.name: add_component
commands.description: Add a component to a GameObject in Unity

properties.description: Initial property values for the component

appliedProperties.description: Array of property names that were successfully set from the properties parameter

commands.name: get_component_types
commands.description: Get available component types in Unity

search.description: Search for component types by name

onlyAddable.description: Return only components that can be added to GameObjects (default: false)

commands.name: list_components
commands.description: List all components on a GameObject in Unity

includeInherited.description: Include inherited base component types (default: false)

success.description: True when components were successfully listed

properties.description: Component property details (only if includeProperties was true in request). May include type-specific fields like 'position', 'rotation', 'scale' for Transform; 'mass', 'drag', 'useGravity' for Rigidbody, etc.

components.description: Array of component descriptors on the GameObject

commands.name: modify_component
commands.description: Modify properties of a component on a GameObject in Unity

componentIndex.description: Index of component if multiple of same type exist (default: 0)

properties.description: Properties to modify with their new values

success.description: True when properties were successfully modified

componentIndex.description: Index of the component that was modified

modifiedProperties.description: Array of property names that were successfully modified

commands.name: remove_component
commands.description: Remove a component from a GameObject in Unity

componentIndex.description: Index of component if multiple of same type exist (default: 0)

removed.description: True if a component was actually removed, false if it didn't exist

componentIndex.description: Index of the removed component (only present when removed=true)

commands.name: clear_console
commands.description: Clear Unity Editor console logs

clearOnPlay.description: Clear console when entering play mode

remainingCount.description: Number of logs remaining after clear

clearOnPlay.description: Clear console when entering play mode

preservedWarnings.description: Number of warnings preserved when clearing (present only when preserving)

preservedErrors.description: Number of errors preserved when clearing (present only when preserving)

commands.name: enhanced_read_logs
commands.description: Read Unity console logs with advanced filtering

count.description: Number of logs to retrieve (1-1000, default: 100)

sinceTimestamp.description: Only return logs after this timestamp (ISO 8601)

untilTimestamp.description: Only return logs before this timestamp (ISO 8601)

groupedLogs.description: Logs grouped by the specified criteria (type, file, or time)

totalCaptured.description: Total number of logs in console
Total number of logs in console

commands.name: manage_layers
commands.description: Manage Unity project layers (add, remove, list, convert)

commands.name: manage_selection
commands.description: Manage Unity Editor selection (get, set, clear)

objectPaths.description: Array of GameObject paths for set action

includeDetails.description: Include detailed information for get action

commands.name: manage_tags
commands.description: Manage Unity project tags (add, remove, list)

action.description: Action to perform: add, remove, or get tags

tagName.description: Name of the tag (required for add and remove actions)

commands.name: manage_tools
commands.description: Manage Unity Editor tools and plugins (list, activate, deactivate, refresh)

toolName.description: Name of the tool (required for activate/deactivate)

category.description: Filter tools by category (optional for get action)

commands.name: manage_windows
commands.description: Manage Unity Editor windows (list, focus, get state)

windowType.description: Type of window (e.g., SceneView, GameView, InspectorWindow)

includeHidden.description: Include hidden/minimized windows in get action

commands.name: create_gameobject
commands.description: Create a GameObject in Unity scene

tag.description: Tag to assign to the GameObject

id.description: Instance ID of the created GameObject

path.description: Hierarchy path to the created GameObject

isActive.description: Whether the GameObject is active in the scene

commands.name: delete_gameobject
commands.description: Delete GameObject(s) from Unity scene

path.description: Path to a single GameObject to delete

paths.description: Array of paths to multiple GameObjects to delete

deleted.description: Array of paths of deleted GameObjects

notFound.description: Array of paths that were not found

commands.name: find_gameobject
commands.description: Find GameObjects in Unity scene by name, tag, or layer

exactMatch.description: Whether to match name exactly (default: true)

objects.description: Array of found GameObjects with their properties

commands.name: get_hierarchy
commands.description: Get the Unity scene hierarchy

maxDepth.description: Maximum depth to traverse (-1 for unlimited, default: -1)

objectCount.description: Number of root GameObjects in hierarchy

children.description: Recursive hierarchy nodes for child GameObjects (respects maxDepth)

hierarchy.description: Hierarchy tree of GameObjects starting from root

error.description: Error message if hierarchy retrieval failed

commands.name: modify_gameobject
commands.description: Modify properties of an existing GameObject

path.description: Path to the GameObject to modify (required)

parentPath.description: Path to new parent GameObject (null to unparent)

id.description: Instance ID of the modified GameObject

modified.description: Whether any properties were actually modified

commands.name: execute_menu_item
commands.description: Execute Unity Editor menu items

action.description: Action to perform: execute menu item or get available menus

parameters.description: Additional parameters for menu execution (if supported)

safetyCheck.description: Enable safety checks to prevent execution of dangerous menu items

commands.name: get_editor_state
commands.description: Get current Unity editor state including play mode status

commands.name: pause_game
commands.description: Pause or resume Unity play mode

commands.name: play_game
commands.description: Start Unity play mode to test the game

commands.name: stop_game
commands.description: Stop Unity play mode and return to edit mode

commands.name: create_scene
commands.description: Create a new scene in Unity

sceneName.description: Name of the scene to create

path.description: Path where the scene should be saved (e.g., "Assets/Scenes/"). If not specified, defaults to "Assets/Scenes/"

loadScene.description: Whether to load the scene after creation (default: true)

addToBuildSettings.description: Whether to add the scene to build settings (default: false)

path.description: Full asset path where the scene was saved

sceneIndex.description: Build settings index if added to build settings, -1 otherwise

isLoaded.description: Whether the scene was loaded after creation

commands.name: get_scene_info
commands.description: Get detailed information about a scene

scenePath.description: Full path to the scene file. If not provided, gets info about current scene.

sceneName.description: Name of the scene. Use either scenePath or sceneName, not both.

includeGameObjects.description: Include list of root GameObjects in the scene (only for loaded scenes). Default: false

scenePath.description: Full asset path to the scene

isActive.description: Whether scene is the active scene

buildIndex.description: Build settings index, -1 if not in build settings

fileSize.description: File size in bytes (present if scene exists on disk, line 645)

lastModified.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last modification (present if scene exists on disk, line 646)

rootGameObjects.description: Root GameObjects in scene (present if includeGameObjects=true and scene is loaded)

rootObjectCount.description: Number of root GameObjects (present if includeGameObjects=true)

totalObjectCount.description: Total GameObjects including children (present if includeGameObjects=true)

summary.description: Human-readable summary of the scene state

commands.name: list_scenes
commands.description: List all scenes in the Unity project

includeLoadedOnly.description: Only include currently loaded scenes (default: false)

includeBuildScenesOnly.description: Only include scenes in build settings (default: false)

includePath.description: Filter scenes by path pattern (e.g., "Levels" to find scenes in Levels folder)

buildIndex.description: Build settings index, -1 if not in build settings

isActive.description: Whether scene is the active scene

totalCount.description: Total number of scenes matching filters

inBuildCount.description: Number of scenes in build settings

commands.name: load_scene
commands.description: Load a scene in Unity

scenePath.description: Full path to the scene file (e.g., "Assets/Scenes/MainMenu.unity")

sceneName.description: Name of the scene to load (must be in build settings). Use either scenePath or sceneName, not both.

loadMode.description: How to load the scene. Single replaces current scene(s), Additive adds to current scene(s) (default: Single)

scenePath.description: Full asset path to the loaded scene

previousScene.description: Name of previous scene (Single mode only, null for Additive)

activeSceneCount.description: Total active scene count (Additive mode only, null for Single)

commands.name: save_scene
commands.description: Save the current scene in Unity

scenePath.description: Path where to save the scene. If not provided, saves to current scene path. Required if saveAs is true.

saveAs.description: Whether to save as a new scene (creates a copy). Default: false

scenePath.description: Full asset path where scene was saved

originalPath.description: Original scene path (saveAs mode only, null otherwise)

isDirty.description: Whether scene has unsaved changes after save (always false on success)

commands.name: analyze_screenshot
commands.description: Analyze Unity screenshots for content, UI elements, colors, and more

imagePath.description: Path to the screenshot file to analyze (must be within Assets folder)

base64Data.description: Base64 encoded image data (alternative to imagePath)

analysisType.description: Type of analysis: basic (colors, dimensions), ui (UI element detection), content (scene content), full (all)

prompt.description: Optional prompt for AI-based analysis (e.g., "Find all buttons in the UI")

commands.name: capture_screenshot
commands.description: Capture screenshots from Unity Editor (Game View, Scene View, or specific windows)

outputPath.description: Path to save the screenshot (e.g., "Assets/Screenshots/capture.png"). If not provided, auto-generates with timestamp

captureMode.description: What to capture: game (Game View), scene (Scene View), or window (specific editor window)

width.description: Custom width for the screenshot (0 = use current view size)

height.description: Custom height for the screenshot (0 = use current view size)

includeUI.description: Include UI elements in the screenshot (Game View only)

windowName.description: Name of the window to capture (required when captureMode is "window")

encodeAsBase64.description: Return the screenshot data as base64 encoded string

path.description: Path where the screenshot was saved

fileSize.description: Size of the file in bytes

includeUI.description: Whether UI was included (game mode only)

cameraRotation.description: Camera rotation in Euler angles (scene mode only)

base64Data.description: Base64 encoded image data (if encodeAsBase64 was true)

commands.name: create_script
commands.description: Create a new C# script in Unity project

path.description: Directory path where script will be created

commands.name: delete_script
commands.description: Delete a C# script from Unity project

scriptPath.description: Full path to the script file (e.g., Assets/Scripts/PlayerController.cs)

scriptName.description: Name of the script to search for (alternative to scriptPath)

searchPath.description: Directory to search in when using scriptName

createBackup.description: Whether to create a backup before deleting

scriptPath.description: Relative asset path to the deleted script (returned when single script is deleted)

deletedPaths.description: Array of relative paths to deleted scripts (returned when multiple scripts with same name are deleted with force=true)

commands.name: list_scripts
commands.description: List C# scripts in Unity project

pattern.description: Pattern to match script names (supports wildcards like *Controller*)

includeMetadata.description: Include file metadata (size, modification date, etc.)

maxResults.description: Maximum number of results to return

path.description: Relative asset path to the script

type.description: Determined script type (MonoBehaviour, ScriptableObject, Editor, EditorWindow, StaticClass, Interface, or Unknown)

size.description: File size in bytes (included if includeMetadata=true)

lastModified.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last modification (included if includeMetadata=true)

scripts.description: Array of scripts matching search criteria

totalCount.description: Number of scripts in the result array

commands.name: read_script
commands.description: Read the contents of a C# script file

scriptPath.description: Full path to the script file (e.g., Assets/Scripts/PlayerController.cs)

scriptName.description: Name of the script to search for (alternative to scriptPath)

searchPath.description: Directory to search in when using scriptName

includeMetadata.description: Whether to include file metadata (line count, size, etc.)

scriptContent.description: Full text content of the script file

scriptPath.description: Relative asset path to the script

lastModified.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last modification (included if includeMetadata=true)

lineCount.description: Total number of lines in the script (included if includeMetadata=true)

fileSize.description: File size in bytes (included if includeMetadata=true)

commands.name: update_script
commands.description: Update an existing C# script in Unity project

scriptPath.description: Full path to the script file (e.g., Assets/Scripts/PlayerController.cs)

scriptName.description: Name of the script to search for (alternative to scriptPath)

searchPath.description: Directory to search in when using scriptName

updateMode.description: How to update the script content

createBackup.description: Whether to create a backup before updating

scriptPath.description: Relative asset path to the updated script

backupPath.description: Relative path to the backup file (included if createBackup=true)

commands.name: validate_script
commands.description: Validate a C# script for syntax and Unity compatibility

scriptPath.description: Full path to the script file (e.g., Assets/Scripts/PlayerController.cs)

scriptName.description: Name of the script to search for (alternative to scriptPath)

searchPath.description: Directory to search in when using scriptName

isValid.description: Whether the script passed validation (true if errors array is empty)

errors.description: Array of syntax errors found during validation

warnings.description: Array of compatibility warnings (included if checkUnityCompatibility=true)

suggestions.description: Array of code improvement suggestions (included if suggestImprovements=true)

scriptPath.description: Relative asset path to the script being validated (included if validating a file, not inline content)

commands.name: clear_logs
commands.description: Internal editor command (not exposed as an MCP tool).

commands.name: handshake
commands.description: Connect-time handshake: the editor self-describes (protocol version, Unity version, project path, available commands) so the server can detect version/project mismatches and advertise only supported tools. Internal (not exposed as an MCP tool).

availableCommands.description: Command types this editor instance actually dispatches

commands.name: ping
commands.description: Test connection to Unity Editor

echo.description: Echo of the optional 'message' parameter sent in request; null if not provided

commands.name: read_logs
commands.description: Read Unity console logs

count.description: Number of logs to retrieve (1-1000, default: 100)

logType.description: Filter by log type (Log, Warning, Error, Assert, Exception)

count.description: Number of logs returned in this response

totalCaptured.description: Total number of logs captured (may be larger than count)

commands.name: refresh_assets
commands.description: Trigger Unity to refresh assets and check for compilation

isCompiling.description: Whether Unity is currently compiling after refresh

commands.name: cancel_tests
commands.description: Cancel currently running tests in Unity

message.description: Status message describing the cancellation result

wasCancelled.description: Whether tests were successfully cancelled (true) or no tests were running (false)

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when cancellation occurred (present only when wasCancelled is true)

commands.name: get_test_results
commands.description: Get results from the last test run in Unity

includeDetails.description: Include detailed test output, stack traces, and assertion results

filterStatus.description: Filter results by status (Passed, Failed, Skipped, Inconclusive)

message.description: Test output message (only when includeDetails is true)

stackTrace.description: Stack trace for failed tests (only when includeDetails is true)

output.description: Test output text (only when includeDetails is true)

duration.description: Total duration of all tests in seconds

totalTests.description: Total number of test results available

hasResults.description: Whether any results are available (only when no results exist)

commands.name: list_tests
commands.description: List all available tests in the Unity project

testMode.description: Test mode to list (EditMode, PlayMode, or EditAndPlayMode)

filter.description: Filter pattern to match test names

className.description: Full class name containing the test

assemblyName.description: Assembly name where the test is defined

testMode.description: The test mode that was searched (EditMode, PlayMode, or EditAndPlayMode)

commands.name: run_tests
commands.description: Run tests in Unity Test Runner

testMode.description: Test mode to run (EditMode, PlayMode, or EditAndPlayMode)

testNames.description: Specific test names to run (runs all if not specified)

runAll.description: Run all tests in the specified mode

message.description: Status message about the test execution start or filter result

testCount.description: Number of tests specified to run (0 if runAll is true or all are run)

runAll.description: Whether all tests are being run

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when test execution started (present when tests start successfully)

commands.name: click_ui_element
commands.description: Simulate clicking on UI elements

elementPath.description: Full hierarchy path to the UI element

holdDuration.description: Duration to hold click in milliseconds

position.description: Specific position within element to click

commands.name: find_ui_elements
commands.description: Find UI elements in Unity scene by type, tag, or name

elementType.description: Filter by UI component type (Button, Toggle, Slider, etc.)

commands.name: get_ui_element_state
commands.description: Get detailed state information about UI elements

elementPath.description: Full hierarchy path to the UI element

commands.name: set_ui_element_value
commands.description: Set values for UI input elements

elementPath.description: Full hierarchy path to the UI element

value.description: New value to set (type depends on element type)

commands.name: simulate_ui_input
commands.description: Simulate complex UI interactions and input sequences

elementPath.description: Target UI element path (for simple input)

inputType.description: Type of input to simulate (for simple input)

inputData.description: Data for input (e.g., text to type)

inputSequence.description: Array of input actions to perform (for complex input)

commands.name: list_unity_instances
commands.description: List the Unity editor instances currently running and discoverable (project path, Unity version, port). There is no default target — pass an instance from this list as the required "instance" to list_unity_tools / call_unity_tool. Works even when no editor is connected.

includeStale.description: Also include descriptors whose process is gone / heartbeat is stale (for diagnosing a missing editor). Default: false.

instances.description: Discoverable editors with projectPath, unityVersion, port, pid, protocolVersion, host, startedAt, lastHeartbeat, live.

commands.name: list_unity_tools
commands.description: List the tools a connected Unity editor actually supports, with their schemas (learned from the editor at runtime). Discover what call_unity_tool can invoke on a given instance. Returns names + descriptions by default; pass "name" for one tool's full parameter schema AND result-field hints (its response shape — read these to drive call_unity_tool's `fields` projection), or "category" to filter.

instance.description: REQUIRED — the target editor (a project path or port). There is no default instance: every call must name its editor. Use list_unity_instances to see what is running.

category.description: Only return tools in this category (e.g. "gameobject", "scene").

name.description: Return the full {name, category, description, params, result} schema for just this one tool — including its result-field hints (the response shape).

tools.description: Each: {name, category, description}. Omitted when a single 'name' was requested.

tool.description: The full {name, category, description, params, result} for the requested 'name' (result is the response-shape hint for fields projection).

schemasAvailable.description: False when the editor advertises command names only (older package build); call_unity_tool then passes params through unvalidated.

commands.name: call_unity_tool
commands.description: Invoke any tool a connected Unity editor supports, by name (discover names + schemas with list_unity_tools). Params are validated against the editor-advertised schema before the call, so a validation error tells you exactly what to fix. The "instance" (a project path or port) is required — there is no default editor, so every call names its target. To trim the response, pass params.fields — an array of dot-paths (GraphQL-style); omit for the full result.

instance.description: REQUIRED — the target editor (a project path or port). There is no default instance: every call must name its editor. Use list_unity_instances to see what is running.

tool.description: The tool name to invoke (see list_unity_tools).

params.description: Parameters for the tool, matching its advertised schema. Also accepts an optional reserved "fields": a string[] of dot-paths that projects the result to just those fields (e.g. ["count","objects.name","state.isPlaying"]); arrays are transparent (the path applies to each element). Omit for all fields. Discover the shape by calling once without "fields".

result.description: The invoked tool's result, passed through verbatim; its shape depends on the tool (see that tool's result schema).
